title: multiplot -- Software and Academic Style Gallery
text: multiplot lets emulate default plot styles nine statistical graphing software packages plus one academic publication style single function call: ggchoice("style"). ggchoice() call applies three things : complete theme_xxx() — grid lines, borders, fonts, legend position scale_color_xxx() — discrete colour palette matching software scale_fill_xxx() — discrete fill palette matching software Scales additive: user-supplied scale_*() calls added ggchoice() take priority, can always fine-tune. want visual theme default colour/fill scales, use include_scales = FALSE:  package defaults base_family = "sans" portability. Exact Arial Helvetica rendering depends installed system fonts graphics devices.
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy)) +   geom_boxplot(aes(fill = class)) +   ggchoice("prism", include_scales = FALSE) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +   labs(title = "Prism Theme Only + User Fill Scale") ## Scale for fill is already present. ## Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.

title: multiplot -- Software and Academic Style Gallery
text: White background, grid, black box border, sans-serif (Arial) font, bold title. Prism pastel--bold colour defaults.
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy)) +   geom_boxplot_prism(aes(fill = class)) +   ggchoice("prism") +   labs(title = "GraphPad Prism Style")

title: multiplot -- Software and Academic Style Gallery
text: Classic SPSS: white bg, light grey grid, full box border, sans-serif (Arial), muted professional palette SPSS blue (#3E58AC) dominant. Legend right, title centred. Understated, publication-ready.
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy)) +   geom_boxplot_prism(aes(fill = class)) +   ggchoice("spss") +   labs(title = "SPSS Style")

title: multiplot -- Software and Academic Style Gallery
code: df <- data.frame(   group = c("Control", "Treatment A", "Treatment B"),   mean  = c(10, 14, 8),   sd    = c(2, 3, 1.5) )  ggplot(df, aes(group, mean)) +   geom_col_prism(fill = "#5B9BD5") +   geom_errorbar_prism(aes(ymin = mean - sd, ymax = mean + sd)) +   ggchoice("prism") +   labs(title = "Prism T-bar Error Bars", x = "", y = "Value")

title: multiplot -- Software and Academic Style Gallery
text: User scales added ggchoice() take priority:
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy)) +   geom_boxplot_prism(aes(fill = class)) +   ggchoice("prism") +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +   # overrides Prism fill   labs(title = "Prism Theme + Custom Brewer Fill") ## Scale for fill is already present. ## Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.

title: multiplot -- Software and Academic Style Gallery
text: heatmaps, surface plots, continuous-colour visualization, use _c suffix variants:   Available continuous scales: scale_color/fill_prism_c(), scale_color/fill_origin_c(), scale_color/fill_matlab_c(), scale_color/fill_stata_c(), scale_color/fill_academic_c(), scale_color/fill_minitab_c(), scale_color/fill_medcalc_c().
code: # Simulated expression matrix set.seed(42) dm <- data.frame(   gene  = rep(LETTERS[1:20], each = 10),   sample = rep(1:10, 20),   expr   = rnorm(200) )  # MATLAB-style heatmap (Parula colormap) ggplot(dm, aes(sample, gene, fill = expr)) +   geom_tile() +   ggchoice("matlab") +   scale_fill_matlab_c() +   labs(title = "MATLAB Parula Heatmap", x = "Sample", y = "Gene") ## Scale for fill is already present. ## Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale. # Academic grayscale heatmap ggplot(dm, aes(sample, gene, fill = expr)) +   geom_tile() +   ggchoice("academic") +   scale_fill_academic_c() +   labs(title = "Academic Grayscale Heatmap", x = "Sample", y = "Gene") ## Scale for fill is already present. ## Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.

title: multiplot -- Software and Academic Style Gallery

text: Calling ggchoice() argument (ggchoice("ggplot2")) returns plain theme_bw() — scale changes applied. GraphPad Prism, SPSS, OriginPro, Stata, SigmaPlot, JMP, MATLAB, Minitab, MedCalc trademarks product names respective owners. multiplot independent affiliated endorsed owners.

what: Emulate Statistical Plot Styles of Nine Software Packages and an Academic Style
title: Emulate Statistical Plot Styles of Nine Software Packages and an Academic Style
text: Emulate default statistical plot styles nine graphing software packages plus one academic publication style R single function call — built top ggplot2.
code: library(ggplot2) library(multiplot)  ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy)) +   geom_boxplot(aes(fill = class)) +   ggchoice("prism")   # one call = theme + colour + fill

title: Emulate Statistical Plot Styles of Nine Software Packages and an Academic Style
text: One API many software looks: ggchoice() applies theme, colour scale, fill scale, axis details together. 10 familiar visual styles: Prism, SPSS, OriginPro, Stata, SigmaPlot, JMP, MATLAB, Minitab, MedCalc, Academic. Publication-friendly helpers: Prism-style columns, error bars, boxplots, comparison annotations. Still pure ggplot2: user-added scales ggchoice() keep priority, every style remains easy customize. Theme-mode: ggchoice("prism", include_scales = FALSE) applies theme without replacing user colour/fill scales.

title: Emulate Statistical Plot Styles of Nine Software Packages and an Academic Style
text: Every statistical graphing software (GraphPad Prism, SPSS, OriginPro, Stata, MATLAB, …) distinctive default visual style. move software, plots look different. publish R, reviewers often expect specific “look.” multiplot solves bundling software-inspired visual style one function: ggchoice(). "academic" style additional publication-oriented benchmark rather real software default.

title: Emulate Statistical Plot Styles of Nine Software Packages and an Academic Style
text: package demo script generates ten figures used companion F1000Research Software Tool Article multiplot installed: GitHub-install workflow: local development repository: demo installs multiplot GitHub package already available, remove overwrite user’s global R library.

title: Emulate Statistical Plot Styles of Nine Software Packages and an Academic Style
text: Direct screenshot checks currently cover Prism, SPSS, MATLAB bar/box plots. Additional screenshot template comparisons cover JMP, MedCalc, Minitab, OriginPro, SigmaPlot, Stata selected plot types. package therefore aims practical style emulation, pixel-perfect reproduction every commercial software output. Evidence levels used manuscript:

title: Emulate Statistical Plot Styles of Nine Software Packages and an Academic Style

title: Emulate Statistical Plot Styles of Nine Software Packages and an Academic Style
code: library(ggplot2) library(multiplot)  p <- ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy)) + geom_boxplot(aes(fill = class))  # Switch style with one call p + ggchoice("prism")     # GraphPad Prism look p + ggchoice("spss")      # SPSS look p + ggchoice("origin")    # OriginPro look p + ggchoice("stata")     # Stata s2color look p + ggchoice("matlab")    # MATLAB R2014b+ look p + ggchoice()            # default theme_bw()

title: Emulate Statistical Plot Styles of Nine Software Packages and an Academic Style
text: Scales additive — user calls ggchoice() take priority: expected ggplot2 message may appear intentionally replace default scale: Scale fill already present. Adding another scale.... Use include_scales = FALSE want theme default colour/fill scales:
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy)) +   geom_boxplot(aes(fill = class)) +   ggchoice("prism") +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2")   # your colour choice wins ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy)) +   geom_boxplot(aes(fill = class)) +   ggchoice("prism", include_scales = FALSE) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2")

title: Emulate Statistical Plot Styles of Nine Software Packages and an Academic Style
text: T-bar error bars + Prism-style columns: Statistical comparison annotations (requires ggpubr):
code: df <- data.frame(group = c("A","B"), mean = c(10, 15), sd = c(2, 3)) ggplot(df, aes(group, mean)) +   geom_col_prism(fill = "#5B9BD5") +   geom_errorbar_prism(aes(ymin = mean - sd, ymax = mean + sd)) +   ggchoice("prism") ggplot(ToothGrowth, aes(supp, len)) +   geom_boxplot(aes(fill = supp)) +   ggchoice("prism") +   stat_compare_means_prism(comparisons = list(c("OJ", "VC")))

title: Emulate Statistical Plot Styles of Nine Software Packages and an Academic Style
text: Discrete palettes interpolate number groups exceeds source palette length. Shape scales repeat marker sequence rep_len().

title: Emulate Statistical Plot Styles of Nine Software Packages and an Academic Style
text: heatmaps continuous data, append _c scale name: Available: prism_c, origin_c, matlab_c, stata_c, academic_c, minitab_c, medcalc_c (14 functions).
code: ggplot(volcano_df, aes(Var1, Var2, fill = value)) +   geom_tile() +   ggchoice("matlab") +   scale_fill_matlab_c()      # Parula-style continuous gradient

title: Emulate Statistical Plot Styles of Nine Software Packages and an Academic Style
text: Theme always applies, scales overridable. ggchoice() returns list theme() + scale_color() + scale_fill(). ggplot2’s + “later wins,” scale_*() add ggchoice() takes priority. Font fallback generic. Style documentation names familiar fonts Arial Helvetica, package defaults base_family = "sans" cross-platform portability. Users need exact font rendering install target font pass base_family, export font-aware device ragg. ggplot2 internals modified. themes inherit theme_bw() theme_classic(). scales use discrete_scale(). Safe use ggplot2 extension. Palettes software-derived approximations. See vignette inst/paper/verification_log.md evidence level version notes. Runtime dependencies light. Core plotting requires ggplot2. ggpubr optional stat_compare_means_prism(), survival used demo, colorspace/farver analysis-dependencies compliance benchmark.

title: Emulate Statistical Plot Styles of Nine Software Packages and an Academic Style

title: Prism-style boxplot — geom_boxplot_prism
text: wrapper around geom_boxplot Prism defaults: black border, compact width, hidden outliers.  Intended paired ggchoice("prism") authentic GraphPad Prism appearance.

title: Prism-style boxplot — geom_boxplot_prism
code: geom_boxplot_prism(   mapping = NULL,   data = NULL,   width = 0.6,   outlier.shape = NA,   ... )

title: Prism-style boxplot — geom_boxplot_prism
text: mapping Set aesthetic mappings. data data displayed. width Box width (default 0.6). outlier.shape Shape outliers (NA = hidden, Prism default). ... arguments passed geom_boxplot.

title: Prism-style boxplot — geom_boxplot_prism
code: library(ggplot2) #> Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.4.3 ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy)) +   geom_boxplot_prism(aes(fill = class)) +   ggchoice("prism")

title: Prism-style column bars — geom_col_prism
text: wrapper around geom_col Prism defaults: solid fill, thin black border, compact bar width.

title: Prism-style column bars — geom_col_prism
code: geom_col_prism(   mapping = NULL,   data = NULL,   width = 0.7,   color = "black",   linewidth = 0.3,   ... )

title: Prism-style column bars — geom_col_prism
text: mapping Set aesthetic mappings created ggplot2::aes(). data data displayed layer. width Bar width (default 0.7). color Border colour (default "black"). linewidth Border line width mm (default 0.3). ... arguments passed geom_col.

title: Prism-style column bars — geom_col_prism
code: library(ggplot2) df <- data.frame(x = c("A", "B"), y = c(10, 15)) ggplot(df, aes(x, y)) +   geom_col_prism(fill = "#5B9BD5") +   ggchoice("prism")

title: Prism-style T-bar error bars — geom_errorbar_prism
text: wrapper around geom_errorbar defaults tuned match GraphPad Prism's T-bar error bar appearance (wider cap width consistent line weight).

title: Prism-style T-bar error bars — geom_errorbar_prism
code: geom_errorbar_prism(   mapping = NULL,   data = NULL,   width = 0.3,   linewidth = 0.5,   ... )

title: Prism-style T-bar error bars — geom_errorbar_prism
text: mapping Set aesthetic mappings created ggplot2::aes(). data data displayed layer. width Width error bar cap native data units (default 0.3). linewidth Line width mm (default 0.5). ... arguments passed geom_errorbar.

title: Prism-style T-bar error bars — geom_errorbar_prism
code: library(ggplot2) df <- data.frame(x = c("A", "B"), y = c(10, 15), sd = c(2, 3)) ggplot(df, aes(x, y)) +   geom_col_prism(fill = "#5B9BD5") +   geom_errorbar_prism(aes(ymin = y - sd, ymax = y + sd))

what: Apply a software-specific plot style — ggchoice
title: Apply a software-specific plot style — ggchoice
text: One-stop function applies complete visual style target graphing software ggplot2 plot.  style bundles theme, colour scale, fill scale.  Scales additive: user-supplied scales added ggchoice() take priority.

title: Apply a software-specific plot style — ggchoice
code: ggchoice(   style = c("ggplot2", "prism", "spss", "origin", "stata", "academic", "sigmaplot",     "jmp", "matlab", "minitab", "medcalc"),   base_size = 12,   base_family = NULL,   axis_offset = FALSE,   include_scales = TRUE,   ... )

title: Apply a software-specific plot style — ggchoice
text: style Character string naming target software.  Supported values: "prism", "spss", "origin", "stata", "academic", "sigmaplot", "jmp", "matlab", "minitab", "medcalc".  missing "ggplot2", returns theme_bw() (scale change). base_size Base font size pts (default 12). base_family Base font family.  styles default "sans" (Arial/Helvetica). axis_offset TRUE, apply axis offset expansion create characteristic Prism/SPSS axis separation gap.  effective "prism" "spss" styles; ignored others. Default FALSE. include_scales TRUE (default), return style theme plus default colour fill scales. FALSE, return theme requested axis-offset scales, allowing advanced users keep colour/fill scales. ... Ignored (reserved future use).

title: Apply a software-specific plot style — ggchoice
text: list ggplot2 theme scale objects can added ggplot +, single theme "ggplot2".

title: Apply a software-specific plot style — ggchoice
code: library(ggplot2)  # Prism-style boxplot ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy)) +   geom_boxplot(aes(fill = class)) +   ggchoice("prism")   # Override fill scale after ggchoice ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy)) +   geom_boxplot(aes(fill = class)) +   ggchoice("prism") +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.   # Default ggplot2 ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy)) +   geom_boxplot(aes(fill = class)) +   ggchoice()   # Prism with axis offset: ggchoice("prism", axis_offset = TRUE)  # Theme-only Prism look, keeping user-defined scales ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy)) +   geom_boxplot(aes(fill = class)) +   ggchoice("prism", include_scales = FALSE) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.

code: scale_shape_prism(...)  scale_shape_spss(...)  scale_shape_origin(...)  scale_shape_stata(...)  scale_shape_academic(...)  scale_shape_sigmaplot(...)  scale_shape_jmp(...)  scale_shape_matlab(...)  scale_shape_minitab(...)  scale_shape_medcalc(...)

title: Prism-style statistical comparison annotations — stat_compare_means_prism
text: Wraps stat_compare_means defaults tuned match GraphPad Prism's significance bracket formatting: P-value cutoffs 0.05 / 0.01 / 0.001 / 0.0001, Prism-style label formatting, bracket positioning appropriate column / bar charts.

title: Prism-style statistical comparison annotations — stat_compare_means_prism
code: stat_compare_means_prism(   comparisons,   method = "t.test",   p.format = c("prism", "signif", "numeric", "scientific"),   label = c("bracket", "middle"),   tip.length = 0.02,   step.increase = 0.12,   hide.ns = FALSE,   ... )

title: Prism-style statistical comparison annotations — stat_compare_means_prism
text: comparisons list length-2 vectors specifying groups compare (e.g. list(c("","B"), c("B","C"))). method Statistical test method. One "t.test", "wilcox.test", "anova", "kruskal.test". Default "t.test". p.format display p-values. "prism" (default) shows exact P value "P < 0.0001"; "signif" shows significance stars ; "numeric" shows raw p-value; "scientific" uses scientific notation. label Position p-value label along bracket. "bracket" (default) places label bracket; "middle" places bracket. tip.length Tip length comparison brackets native units (default 0.02). step.increase Vertical spacing increment stacked brackets (default 0.12). hide.ns TRUE, hide "ns" (non-significant) annotations. Default FALSE. ... arguments passed stat_compare_means.

title: Prism-style statistical comparison annotations — stat_compare_means_prism
text: ggplot2 layer (invisibly).

title: Prism-style statistical comparison annotations — stat_compare_means_prism
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggplot2) ggplot(ToothGrowth, aes(supp, len)) +   geom_boxplot(aes(fill = supp)) +   ggchoice("prism") +   stat_compare_means_prism(comparisons = list(c("OJ", "VC"))) } # }
